title: Fixed-Length, Close-Delimited, Absent, and Incomplete Content · Go Semantics Case: Close Delimited Response
summary: A body reader allocates from attacker Content-Length, treats premature EOF as success, consumes the next pipelined message, and reuses a connection after incomplete content. Planned artifact: Add and verify the close delimited response increment in bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use close-delimited response content only in permitted response contexts and mark the connection non-reusable.
Stop after exactly the framed content and retain buffered residual bytes for the next message.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Close Delimited Response · Theory Close Delimited Response
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 9399 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate exact-length, incomplete, and response-only close-delimited content; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use close-delimited response content only in permitted response contexts and mark the connection non-reusable.
